title: Use shipped zlib if the system version is bad
author: 90baf024-6604-450d-8341-d796fe6858f3
body:
BPO | [6016](https://bugs.python.org/issue6016) --- | :--- Nosy | @devdanzin Files |
  • [zlib_setup.diff](https://bugs.python.org/file13980/zlib_setup.diff "Uploaded as text/plain at 2009-05-14.02:05:42 by @devdanzin"): Patch against trunk, seems to apply fine on py3k.

comments.author: 90baf024-6604-450d-8341-d796fe6858f3
comments.body:
The attached patch makes setup.py use the shipped zlib.h in
[Modules/zlib/](https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/main/Modules/zlib) when a bogus zlib.h is found by find_file.

However, I couldn't test it (nor make it work) when a system zlib is
missing (no time right now). Can we build the shipped zlib and link
against it?

comments.author: 90baf024-6604-450d-8341-d796fe6858f3
comments.body:
We'd be asking for trouble with this: incompatible shipped and system
versions or having to build the shipped version. All that for working
around the rare case where the user doesn't have a valid zlib makes no
sense :)
